note: Ground-truth gaps/risks genuinely present in each input. A gap is 'caught' if the output surfaces it as a flag, risk, open item, ownerless/dateless note, contradiction, or implied action — anywhere in the output. Recall = caught / total.

01.desc: updating the customer-facing changelog has no owner
'lock the design system' has no date but blocks eng
is the $40k this quarter or does it roll over — stated as unclear/both
does legal need to review the new checkout copy — nobody knew
feature flag for search rollout — Lena thinks yes, not decided
author said they'll nudge Ravi on the design system — implied action, not on a list

03.desc: AWS reserved instances expire end of month — cost risk if missed
cancel analytics vendor before the 28th renewal
reply to investor with runway numbers before Monday
two-tier vs single price — not decided
hire a designer vs contract it out — leaning but not decided

07.desc: Meadowbrook champion left the company — deal at risk
bring in a solutions engineer for Meadowbrook re-engagement — undecided
light on Q3 pipeline — needs lead gen
Fatima deal legal review sitting 8 days — stalling

08.desc: renewal auto-charges on the 20th — hard deadline
only 2 of 8 users adopted — low adoption is the root risk
Nadia committed to build ROI case + pull usage data by the 18th
budget cut 30%, CFO sees no ROI — churn driver

10.desc: how are split deals credited in new segmentation — unclear
what happens to in-flight deals when territories change — leadership to clarify
is the analytics beta ready for customer demos or internal only — open
anxiety about quota raise and territory redraw — morale risk

12.desc: insurer offered $8k lowball; client should not sign
statute of limitations is two years — time constraint
client to get medical records and police report — needed to proceed
must not give recorded statement / sign anything to the insurer

13.desc: $340k AR over 90 days, mostly Delticom — collection risk
raising prices to offset revenue miss — deferred to next month, board-level
revenue missed forecast by 6% on slipped deals
$200k over on cloud costs — needs the 15% optimization to land

20.desc: Marcus quoted 4-week migration but solutions eng said 6-8 with custom schema
SSO only on the $7k enterprise tier vs $3k pro — forced upsell / cost risk
author should get a second quote from the competitor before committing
loop in security and get the SSO requirement in writing
current contract expires — decision time-boxed to end of month
is enterprise/SSO actually necessary or can they live without it — unresolved
